title: One song earned $2 million a year — for a birthday #Shorts
desc: For decades a music company quietly collected about $2 million a year in royalties on 'Happy Birthday to You' — roughly $5,000 every single day. Films paid thousands just to show a birthday cake, until a 2015 court ruled the copyright invalid and $14 million was refunded. Today the world's most recognized song is finally in the public domain.
